title: AddingInterventionNodes

lean_statement.name: subset_extendingCDMGWithInterventionNodes_J_union_V_of_subset
lean_statement.code:
-- helper required to state the chain-1 rows
/-- Chain-1 helper: if `W‚āā ‚äÜ G.J ‚ą™ G.V`, then `Sum.inl '' W‚āā` is
contained in the node-set union of `G.extendingCDMGWithInterventionNodes
W‚āĀ hW‚āĀ`. Discharges the outer-`ext` precondition for
`((G.ext W‚āĀ hW‚āĀ).ext (Sum.inl '' W‚āā) _)` in chain 1. -/
theorem subset_extendingCDMGWithInterventionNodes_J_union_V_of_subset
    {G : CDMG őĪ} {W‚āĀ W‚āā : Set őĪ} (hW‚āĀ : W‚āĀ ‚äÜ G.J ‚ą™ G.V)
    (hW‚āā : W‚āā ‚äÜ G.J ‚ą™ G.V) :
    Sum.inl '' W‚āā ‚äÜ
      (G.extendingCDMGWithInterventionNodes W‚āĀ hW‚āĀ).J ‚ą™
        (G.extendingCDMGWithInterventionNodes W‚āĀ hW‚āĀ).V := by

lean_statement.name: subset_hardInterventionOn_J_union_V_of_disjoint
lean_statement.code:
-- helper required to state the chain-2 row
/-- Chain-2 helper: if `W‚āĀ ‚äÜ G.J ‚ą™ G.V` and `Disjoint W‚āĀ W‚āā`, then
`W‚āĀ ‚äÜ (G.hardInterventionOn W‚āā).J ‚ą™ (G.hardInterventionOn W‚āā).V`.
Discharges the outer-`ext` precondition for
`((G.hardInterventionOn W‚āā).ext W‚āĀ _)` in chain 2. -/
theorem subset_hardInterventionOn_J_union_V_of_disjoint
    {G : CDMG őĪ} {W‚āĀ W‚āā : Set őĪ} (hW‚āĀ : W‚āĀ ‚äÜ G.J ‚ą™ G.V)
    (hdisj : Disjoint W‚āĀ W‚āā) :
    W‚āĀ ‚äÜ (G.hardInterventionOn W‚āā).J ‚ą™ (G.hardInterventionOn W‚āā).V := by

lean_statement.name: extendingCDMGWithInterventionNodes_fusion_equiv
lean_statement.code:
/-- claim_3_14 chain 1, part 1/2 (fusion lemma): iterating
`extendingCDMGWithInterventionNodes` on disjoint `W‚āĀ`, `W‚āā` is
`CDMGEquiv`-equivalent to a single extension on `W‚āĀ ‚ą™ W‚āā`. Mirrors
the second `=` of chain 1 in the LN's `\Lem` at
`lecture-notes/lecture_notes/graphs.tex` line 836:
`(G_{do(I_{W‚āĀ})})_{do(I_{W‚āā})} = G_{do(I_{W‚āĀ ‚ą™ W‚āā})}`. -/
noncomputable def extendingCDMGWithInterventionNodes_fusion_equiv
    {G : CDMG őĪ} {W‚āĀ W‚āā : Set őĪ}
    (hW‚āĀ : W‚āĀ ‚äÜ G.J ‚ą™ G.V) (hW‚āā : W‚āā ‚äÜ G.J ‚ą™ G.V)
    (hdisj : Disjoint W‚āĀ W‚āā) :
    CDMGEquiv
      ((G.extendingCDMGWithInterventionNodes W‚āĀ hW‚āĀ).extendingCDMGWithInterventionNodes
          (Sum.inl '' W‚āā)
          (subset_extendingCDMGWithInterventionNodes_J_union_V_of_subset hW‚āĀ hW‚āā))
      (G.extendingCDMGWithInterventionNodes (W‚āĀ ‚ą™ W‚āā)
          (Set.union_subset hW‚āĀ hW‚āā)) := by

lean_statement.name: extendingCDMGWithInterventionNodes_comm_equiv
lean_statement.code:
/-- claim_3_14 chain 1, part 2/2 (commute corollary): swapping `W‚āĀ` and
`W‚āā` in the iterated extension is `CDMGEquiv`-symmetric. Mirrors the
first `=` of chain 1:
`(G_{do(I_{W‚āĀ})})_{do(I_{W‚āā})} = (G_{do(I_{W‚āā})})_{do(I_{W‚āĀ})}`. -/
noncomputable def extendingCDMGWithInterventionNodes_comm_equiv
    {G : CDMG őĪ} {W‚āĀ W‚āā : Set őĪ}
    (hW‚āĀ : W‚āĀ ‚äÜ G.J ‚ą™ G.V) (hW‚āā : W‚āā ‚äÜ G.J ‚ą™ G.V)
    (hdisj : Disjoint W‚āĀ W‚āā) :
    CDMGEquiv
      ((G.extendingCDMGWithInterventionNodes W‚āĀ hW‚āĀ).extendingCDMGWithInterventionNodes
          (Sum.inl '' W‚āā)
          (subset_extendingCDMGWithInterventionNodes_J_union_V_of_subset hW‚āĀ hW‚āā))
      ((G.extendingCDMGWithInterventionNodes W‚āā hW‚āā).extendingCDMGWithInterventionNodes
          (Sum.inl '' W‚āĀ)
          (subset_extendingCDMGWithInterventionNodes_J_union_V_of_subset hW‚āā hW‚āĀ)) := by

lean_statement.name: extendingCDMGWithInterventionNodes_hardInterventionOn_comm_equiv
lean_statement.code:
/-- claim_3_14 chain 2: hard intervention on `W‚āā` and extension by
intervention nodes on `W‚āĀ` commute when `Disjoint W‚āĀ W‚āā`. Mirrors the
first `=` of chain 2 in the LN's `\Lem` at
`lecture-notes/lecture_notes/graphs.tex` line 839:
`(G_{do(I_{W‚āĀ})})_{do(W‚āā)} = (G_{do(W‚āā)})_{do(I_{W‚āĀ})}`. -/
noncomputable def extendingCDMGWithInterventionNodes_hardInterventionOn_comm_equiv
    {G : CDMG őĪ} {W‚āĀ W‚āā : Set őĪ}
    (hW‚āĀ : W‚āĀ ‚äÜ G.J ‚ą™ G.V) (hdisj : Disjoint W‚āĀ W‚āā) :
    CDMGEquiv
      ((G.extendingCDMGWithInterventionNodes W‚āĀ hW‚āĀ).hardInterventionOn
          (Sum.inl '' W‚āā))
      ((G.hardInterventionOn W‚āā).extendingCDMGWithInterventionNodes W‚āĀ
          (subset_hardInterventionOn_J_union_V_of_disjoint hW‚āĀ hdisj)) := by

lean_explanation:
The Lean transcription works over `őĪ : Type u`, the ambient vertex type matching the LN's silent assumption that the graph lives over some carrier; `G : CDMG őĪ` is the LN's $G = (J, V, E, L)$, and `W‚āĀ W‚āā : Set őĪ` are the two LN subsets $W_1, W_2 \subseteq J \cup V$. The hypotheses `hW‚āĀ`, `hW‚āā` express the LN's $W_i \subseteq J \cup V$ assumptions, and `hdisj : Disjoint W‚āĀ W‚āā` is the LN's disjointness assumption (Mathlib's order-theoretic `Disjoint`, specialised here to sets).

The LN's $\doit(I_W)$ is the Lean operator `G.extendingCDMGWithInterventionNodes W _`, which mints a fresh intervention-input node $I_w$ for each $w \in W \setminus J$ and rebases the carrier to a `Sum`: `őĪ ‚äē ‚ÜĎ(W \ G.J)`. The LN's $\doit(W)$ (hard intervention) is `G.hardInterventionOn W`, which promotes `W ‚ą© V` into the input layer and is carrier-preserving. Because iterating a carrier-changing operator like `extendingCDMGWithInterventionNodes` produces CDMGs over *different* `Sum`-typed carriers on the two sides of each LN equality, the LN's `=` signs cannot be stated as Lean `Eq` ‚ÄĒ they ship as `CDMGEquiv`, a structure (defined in `TwoDisjointNodeSplittingsCommute.lean` and imported here) bundling a carrier-rewriting `Equiv` plus four `image`-equality fields for `J`, `V`, `E`, and `L`.

Several Mathlib idioms appear in the signatures: `Sum.inl '' W‚āā` is the canonical embedding of the original `W‚āā : Set őĪ` into the extended carrier (the LN's implicit identification of $W_2$ with its image in the new graph); `Set.union_subset hW‚āĀ hW‚āā` combines the two preconditions for the merged third term in chain 1; subtypes like `‚ÜĎ(W‚āĀ \ G.J)` (carrier-level encoding of the LN's $I_j := j$ convention for $j \in J$) appear inside the `Sum`. Implicit `{G W‚āĀ W‚āā}` binders let the conclusion pin down the sets, with the hypotheses explicit.

The row delivers three `CDMGEquiv`-valued declarations ‚ÄĒ chain-1 fusion (collapsing two iterated intervention-node extensions into one on $W_1 \cup W_2$), chain-1 commute (swapping $W_1 \leftrightarrow W_2$, derived from fusion), and chain-2 commute (swapping a hard intervention with an intervention-node extension) ‚ÄĒ plus two named subset helpers (`subset_extendingCDMGWithInterventionNodes_J_union_V_of_subset`, `subset_hardInterventionOn_J_union_V_of_disjoint`) that discharge the type-level preconditions of the iterated extensions.
design_choices:
**`CDMGEquiv` rather than literal `Eq` ‚ÄĒ forced by typing, not stylistic.** Both LN chains read as set-equalities at the CDMG-data level, but the iterated CDMGs live over different carriers: chain 1's iterated side has a nested `Sum` (`(őĪ ‚äē ‚ÜĎ(W‚āĀ \ G.J)) ‚äē ‚ÜĎ(...)`) while its joint third term has a flat `Sum` (`őĪ ‚äē ‚ÜĎ((W‚āĀ ‚ą™ W‚āā) \ G.J)`); chain 2's two sides both have shape `őĪ ‚äē ‚ÜĎ(W‚āĀ \ ?)` but with `?` differing between `G.J` and `G.J ‚ą™ W‚āā`. Under `Disjoint W‚āĀ W‚āā` the two predicates denote the same set, but they are not definitionally equal as predicates, so a literal Lean `Eq` is not type-correct even with the disjointness hypothesis in scope. `CDMGEquiv` (with its `refl`/`symm`/`trans` groupoid laws) is the categorified version: a carrier-rewriting `Equiv` plus four image-equality fields for `J`, `V`, `E`, and `L`. The alternative ‚ÄĒ unfolding the `Sum`s and subtype predicates to bring the carriers into definitional equality ‚ÄĒ would require modifying the underlying `extendingCDMGWithInterventionNodes` definition, far out of scope.

**Fusion + commute split for chain 1, mirroring the LN's "by symmetry" close.** The LN states a chained equality $(G_{\doit(I_{W_1})})_{\doit(I_{W_2})} = (G_{\doit(I_{W_2})})_{\doit(I_{W_1})} = G_{\doit(I_{W_1 \cup W_2})}$ and only proves the *fusion* direction (the second `=`); the first is closed "by symmetry". The Lean factoring matches the LN: `extendingCDMGWithInterventionNodes_fusion_equiv` is the load-bearing fusion lemma, and `extendingCDMGWithInterventionNodes_comm_equiv` is a short corollary composing two fusion calls through a `Set.union_comm` bridge (`Equiv.subtypeEquivRight` absorbing the def-distinct subtype carriers `‚ÜĎ((W‚āĀ ‚ą™ W‚āā) \ G.J)` vs `‚ÜĎ((W‚āā ‚ą™ W‚āĀ) \ G.J)`). Folding the commute into the fusion lemma's conclusion would force every consumer who only wants to swap $W_1 \leftrightarrow W_2$ (without collapsing) to chain two fusion calls themselves at each site, with the union-comm bridge re-derived locally.

**Joint third term materialised for chain 1, but *not* for chain 2.** The LN's chain-1 third term $G_{\doit(I_{W_1 \cup W_2})}$ is a literal single extension on the union ‚ÄĒ the same operator `extendingCDMGWithInterventionNodes` evaluated at `W‚āĀ ‚ą™ W‚āā` ‚ÄĒ so the fusion lemma's right-hand side is a real Lean term in the conclusion. By contrast, the LN's chain-2 third term $G_{\doit(I_{W_1}, W_2)}$ is *notation* for the unambiguous value of either side (as the commented-out LN lines just below the `\Lem` make explicit), not a separate operator. Minting a fresh `do(I_¬∑, ¬∑)` joint operator just to materialise it in Lean would duplicate the API for no payoff: a new CDMG definition with its own four `@[simp]` characterisations, threaded through every downstream caller that currently chains `hardInterventionOn` and `extendingCDMGWithInterventionNodes` directly. The chain-2 `CDMGEquiv` consequently has only the two iterates in its conclusion, and any consumer who wants "the joint value" picks one side by `.symm` if needed.

**Named subset helpers, not inlined preconditions.** Both chains' iterated extensions need to type-check a precondition of shape `W ‚äÜ inner.J ‚ą™ inner.V` where `inner` is itself an `extendingCDMGWithInterventionNodes` (chain 1) or a `hardInterventionOn` (chain 2). Discharging these inline as `by simp; ...` blocks in the headline `CDMGEquiv`-valued signatures would drown the conclusion in tactic noise and force every consumer to re-prove the same subset fact under any goal-state perturbation. The two named helpers ‚ÄĒ `subset_extendingCDMGWithInterventionNodes_J_union_V_of_subset` for chain 1, `subset_hardInterventionOn_J_union_V_of_disjoint` for chain 2 ‚ÄĒ factor this out once. The chain-1 helper does *not* need `Disjoint W‚āĀ W‚āā`, because intervention-node extension is purely additive on the input layer (original nodes survive untouched into the extended graph); the chain-2 helper *does* need disjointness, because hard intervention is a *deletion* on the `V` side (`hardInterventionOn_V = G.V \ W‚āā`), so a vertex `w ‚ąą W‚āĀ ‚ą© G.V ‚ą© W‚āā` would fall out of the extended `V` without disjointness. This is the formal transcription of the LN's implicit $W_1 \cap (V \setminus W_2) = W_1 \cap V$ step under disjointness.

**`Sum.inl ''`-lift on iterated-extension targets.** Each chain's iterated side applies an outer operator (extension or hard intervention) on top of an inner `extendingCDMGWithInterventionNodes W‚āĀ`, whose carrier is `őĪ ‚äē ‚ÜĎ(W‚āĀ \ G.J)`. The outer operator's target set must therefore be a `Set (őĪ ‚äē ‚ÜĎ(W‚āĀ \ G.J))`, not the bare `Set őĪ` of the LN. The LN-faithful lift is `Sum.inl '' W‚āā` ‚ÄĒ the canonical embedding under the convention `Sum.inl = original-őĪ-side` established by the extension construction ‚ÄĒ and any alternative lift would either be type-incorrect or obscure the LN's implicit $\alpha \cong \mathrm{Sum.inl}\,''\,\alpha$ identification.

**Foundation reuse: `CDMGEquiv` imported from `TwoDisjointNodeSplittingsCommute.lean`.** This row is the third consumer of the `CDMGEquiv` structure (after `claim_3_7` and `claim_3_10`). Promoting it to a shared `Section3_1/` foundation file was considered as the natural "third-consumer" trigger, but rejected: the import cost from each consumer is a single line, no fourth consumer is queued in Section 3.2 with a pinned Lean shape, and the reverse move (merging a promoted file back) is cheap if the promotion turns out wrong. The promotion will be revisited if `claim_3_15` or a chapter-4 row needs the same shape.
